header.disclaimer_top: Generated by HealthIQ from commercial laboratory results. Structured discussion aid summarising patterns, hypotheses, and confirmatory testing considerations. Not a clinical diagnosis or treatment recommendation.
header.footer_line: HealthIQ Clinician Summary Report — discussion aid; not diagnostic or prescriptive.

data_quality.confidence_caveat: Given available markers, confidence is constrained by missing or partial reference-range context.

key_findings: signal_homocysteine_elevation_context is suboptimal. homocysteine is the primary driver.
page1.confidence_and_missing_data: Signal confidence 0.90; missing confirmatory markers limit differentiation.

hypotheses.title: B12-associated pattern
hypotheses.summary: Homocysteine is elevated with a pattern that can be consistent with reduced B12-related methylation capacity.
hypotheses.ranking_rationale: Ranked #1 because available evidence; vitamin_b12 counter-evidence.

evidence_against.item: B12 appears clearly within range, which makes a B12-driven pattern less likely on this panel alone.

missing_data.reason: MMA can help evaluate functional B12 deficiency when homocysteine is elevated.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Consider discussing methylmalonic acid (MMA) testing with your clinician to evaluate functional B12 deficiency.

hypotheses.title: Folate-associated pattern
hypotheses.summary: Homocysteine is elevated with a pattern that can be consistent with reduced folate availability for one-carbon metabolism.
hypotheses.ranking_rationale: Ranked #2 because available evidence; limited contradictory evidence.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Consider repeating homocysteine in 8–12 weeks to confirm persistence and track change over time.

hypotheses.title: Inflammatory context pattern
hypotheses.summary: Homocysteine is elevated with inflammatory context signals that may be relevant to vascular stress interpretation.
hypotheses.ranking_rationale: Ranked #3 because crp evidence; limited contradictory evidence.

evidence_for.item: A systemic inflammation signal fired, indicating inflammatory context on this panel.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Consider repeating CRP with a consistent method to see if inflammation markers are persistently elevated.
Consider repeating homocysteine in 8–12 weeks to confirm persistence and track change over time.

hypotheses.title: Renal clearance context pattern
hypotheses.summary: Homocysteine is elevated and renal clearance context may be relevant when interpreting metabolite accumulation patterns.
hypotheses.ranking_rationale: Ranked #4 because available evidence; creatinine counter-evidence.

evidence_against.item: Creatinine appears within range, which reduces evidence for a renal clearance contribution on this panel alone.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Consider repeating homocysteine in 8–12 weeks to confirm persistence and track change over time.
Consider discussing methylmalonic acid (MMA) testing with your clinician to evaluate functional B12 deficiency.
